title: F1 Car — Aerodynamics & DRS
prompt: Walk me through how this F1 car's aerodynamics work — especially the DRS.

sources.title: How airflow makes the car stick
sources.body: Front wing turns flow inboard and downward. Sidepods + floor channel high-velocity air through Venturi tunnels under the car (ground-effect), creating low pressure beneath the floor. Rear wing + beam wing generate the remaining ~40% of downforce. DRS opens a flap in the upper rear wing on straights, dumping that downforce in exchange for top-end speed.

sources.name: F1 Car
sources.finding: Hero geometry. Stays on the table as the reference object the airflow + every callout anchors to.

sources.name: Front Wing
sources.finding: Multi-element wing + endplates. Turns oncoming air downward and outboard around the front tyres. Sets the entire aerodynamic balance of the car — every other surface downstream depends on it.

sources.name: Floor / Venturi Tunnels
sources.finding: Two underbody Venturi tunnels accelerate air, creating low pressure that sucks the car to the track. Source of ~60% of total downforce in the current regulations.

sources.name: Sidepod Inlets
sources.finding: Feed the engine + ERS coolers. Surface geometry above the inlets steers air over the bodywork toward the rear wing and prevents stagnation behind the front tyres.

sources.name: Rear Wing — DRS Flap
sources.finding: Two-element rear wing. Upper element is the DRS flap — hydraulically opens on activation, stalling the wing and shedding ~15% of total drag for a ~9 m/s top-end gain. Closes the moment the driver brakes or lifts.

sources.name: Beam Wing
sources.finding: Lower aerodynamic element between the diffuser and the rear wing. Recovers air from under the floor and helps load the diffuser at high speed.

sources.title: Airflow — DRS closed
sources.description: Stream lines wrap the body, stagnate on the rear wing's upper element, generate the full downforce package.

sources.title: Airflow — DRS open
sources.description: Upper rear-wing element rotates up; the stagnation point disappears; the wing stalls and the air shoots straight through, shedding downforce for top-end speed.

sources.title: DRS — when it's available
sources.finding: DRS is only allowed in designated zones, and only when the driver is within 1.0 s of the car ahead at the detection point. Closes the moment the brake pedal is touched or the throttle is lifted.

options.label: Show DRS in detail
options.detail: Strip the scene to the rear wing + DRS flap with leader line + airflow comparison.

options.label: Floor / ground effect
options.detail: Highlight the underbody Venturi tunnels and the air path beneath the car.

options.label: Front wing
options.detail: Walk through how the front wing sets the rest of the car's aero balance.
